content.text:
Advanced Databases
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF11  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
This module focuses on the essential concepts and technologies for the design, management and exploitation of modern database systems, covering both theoretical and practical aspects.
Recommended prior knowledge:
Basic database concepts, SQL language, relational algebra.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Databases
Review of fundamental concepts: relational model, DBMS, query languages
Types of databases: relational, NoSQL, object-oriented, etc.
DBMS architecture: client-server, distributed architectures
Transaction concepts, concurrency, and data recovery
Advanced SQL Programming
SQL (review): joins, subqueries, aggregate functions, views, stored procedures
Advanced SQL: Triggers, functions, error handling and management
NoSQL data manipulation languages: JSONiq, Cypher
The Object-Relational Model
Presentation of the Object model
Presentation of the Relational-Object model
Concepts of the RO model (complex types, inheritance…)
Querying Object-Relational databases (SQL3)
NoSQL Databases
Introduction to NoSQL databases: types (key-value, document, graph, column)
NoSQL data models: advantages and disadvantages
Case studies: MongoDB, Cassandra, Neo4j
Distributed Databases
Distributed systems architecture
Replication and partitioning techniques
Data consistency in distributed environments: ACID vs BASE
Cloud Databases
Service models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Cloud computing and databases
Security considerations in the cloud
Database Performance and Security
SQL query optimisation
Indexing and access strategies
Security: access control, data encryption, auditing
Security management in distributed databases
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
R. Elmasri & S. Navathe (2016).
Fundamentals of Database Systems
, 7th Edition, Pearson.
T. Connolly & C. Begg (2014).
Database Systems: A Practical Approach to Design, Implementation, and Management
, 6th Edition, Pearson.
Christian Soutou (2013).
SQL pour Oracle
. Editions Eyrolles.
Silberschatz, H. Korth & S. Sudarshan (2019).
Database System Concepts
, 7th Edition, McGraw-Hill.
R.G.G. Cattell (1994).
Object Data Management
. Addison-Wesley.
P. Selmer (2012).
NOSQL stores and Data analytics tools
. Advances in Data Management.
